title: crataegus monogyna subsp. polyacantha
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. polyacantha is a subspecies of the hawthorn tree, a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny branches and white or pink flowers. The medicinal properties of Crataegus monogyna subsp. polyacantha include its high content of flavonoids, anthocyanins, and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective effects. The health benefits of this plant include lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and reducing the risk of heart disease, as well as its potential to improve blood lipid profiles and reduce oxidative stress. Crataegus monogyna subsp. polyacantha can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to support heart health and overall well-being.
